options.content:
# 🔭 CelestiaOps Exoplanet Observatory
Real-time catalog of **confirmed exoplanets** sourced from the NASA Exoplanet Archive (TAP). Data ingested daily at 02:00 UTC. Earth baseline: radius = 1 R⊕, mass = 1 M⊕, orbital period = 365.25 days, equilibrium temperature ≈ 255 K (effective) / 288 K (surface). Habitable zone defined as equilibrium temperature 180 – 310 K.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM exoplanets
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM exoplanets WHERE is_potentially_habitable = true
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT hostname) AS value FROM exoplanets WHERE st_teff BETWEEN 5200 AND 6000
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT hostname) AS value FROM exoplanets WHERE sy_pnum >= 2
SELECT ROUND((MIN(sy_dist) * 3.26156)::numeric, 2) AS value FROM exoplanets WHERE sy_dist IS NOT NULL
SELECT TO_CHAR(last_sync_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC', 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI') AS value FROM sync_state WHERE dag_id = 'ingest_exoplanets'

panels.title: Planet Classification by Radius
panels.description: Rocky < 1.5 R⊕ | Super-Earth 1.5–2.5 | Sub-Neptune 2.5–4 | Neptune-like 4–10 | Gas Giant > 10

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN pl_rade < 1.5 THEN '🪨 Rocky (<1.5 R⊕)' WHEN pl_rade < 2.5 THEN '🌍 Super-Earth (1.5–2.5)' WHEN pl_rade < 4.0 THEN '🌫 Sub-Neptune (2.5–4)' WHEN pl_rade < 10.0 THEN '🔵 Neptune-like (4–10)' ELSE '🪐 Gas Giant (>10)' END AS "Type", COUNT(*) AS "Count" FROM exoplanets WHERE pl_rade IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

SELECT CASE WHEN st_teff < 3700 THEN 'M — Red Dwarf (<3700 K)' WHEN st_teff < 5200 THEN 'K — Orange (3700–5200 K)' WHEN st_teff < 6000 THEN 'G — Sun-like (5200–6000 K)' WHEN st_teff < 7500 THEN 'F — Yellow-White (6000–7500 K)' ELSE 'A+ — Hot (>7500 K)' END AS "Spectral Type", COUNT(*) AS "Count" FROM exoplanets WHERE st_teff IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
SELECT discoverymethod AS "Method", COUNT(*) AS "Count" FROM exoplanets WHERE discoverymethod IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
SELECT disc_year::text AS "Year", COUNT(*) AS "New Planets Confirmed" FROM exoplanets WHERE disc_year IS NOT NULL GROUP BY disc_year ORDER BY disc_year
SELECT make_date(disc_year::int, 6, 1) AS "time", SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (ORDER BY disc_year) AS "Cumulative Planets" FROM exoplanets WHERE disc_year IS NOT NULL GROUP BY disc_year ORDER BY disc_year

SELECT pl_name AS "Planet", hostname AS "Host Star", disc_year AS "Year", discoverymethod AS "Method", ROUND(pl_rade::numeric, 2) AS "Radius (R⊕)", ROUND(pl_masse::numeric, 2) AS "Mass (M⊕)", ROUND(pl_orbper::numeric, 2) AS "Period (days)", ROUND(pl_eqt::numeric, 0) AS "Eq. Temp (K)", ROUND(sy_dist::numeric, 1) AS "Dist (pc)", ROUND((sy_dist * 3.26156)::numeric, 1) AS "Dist (ly)", sy_pnum AS "# Planets in System", CASE WHEN is_potentially_habitable THEN 'HZ' ELSE '' END AS "HZ" FROM exoplanets ORDER BY disc_year DESC, pl_name LIMIT 2000

panels.title: 🌱 Habitable Zone Candidates — Ordered by Earth Temperature Similarity
panels.description: Planets with equilibrium temperature 180–310 K, closest to Earth's ~288 K surface average

targets.rawSql: SELECT pl_name AS "Planet", hostname AS "Star", ROUND(pl_rade::numeric, 2) AS "Radius (R⊕)", ROUND(pl_masse::numeric, 2) AS "Mass (M⊕)", ROUND(pl_eqt::numeric, 0) AS "Eq. Temp (K)", ROUND((pl_eqt - 288)::numeric, 0) AS "ΔT vs Earth (K)", ROUND(sy_dist::numeric, 1) AS "Dist (pc)", ROUND((sy_dist * 3.26156)::numeric, 1) AS "Dist (ly)", disc_year AS "Year", discoverymethod AS "Method" FROM exoplanets WHERE is_potentially_habitable = true ORDER BY ABS(COALESCE(pl_eqt, 9999) - 288), pl_name

SELECT pl_name AS "Planet", hostname AS "Star", ROUND((sy_dist * 3.26156)::numeric, 3) AS "Distance (ly)", ROUND(sy_dist::numeric, 3) AS "Distance (pc)", ROUND(pl_rade::numeric, 2) AS "Radius (R⊕)", ROUND(pl_masse::numeric, 2) AS "Mass (M⊕)", ROUND(pl_eqt::numeric, 0) AS "Eq. Temp (K)", disc_year AS "Year", CASE WHEN is_potentially_habitable THEN 'HZ' ELSE '' END AS "HZ" FROM exoplanets WHERE sy_dist IS NOT NULL ORDER BY sy_dist ASC LIMIT 30

panels.title: Planet Radius vs Mass — Size Classification Map
panels.description: Earth = (1, 1). Neptune ≈ (3.9, 17). Saturn ≈ (9.4, 95). Jupiter ≈ (11.2, 318). Filter: radius < 20 R⊕, mass < 500 M⊕

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(pl_rade::numeric, 3) AS "Radius (R⊕)", ROUND(pl_masse::numeric, 3) AS "Mass (M⊕)", CASE WHEN pl_rade < 1.5 THEN 'Rocky' WHEN pl_rade < 2.5 THEN 'Super-Earth' WHEN pl_rade < 4.0 THEN 'Sub-Neptune' WHEN pl_rade < 10.0 THEN 'Neptune-like' ELSE 'Gas Giant' END AS "Type" FROM exoplanets WHERE pl_rade IS NOT NULL AND pl_masse IS NOT NULL AND pl_rade < 20 AND pl_masse < 500 ORDER BY pl_rade

SELECT hostname AS "Star System", MAX(sy_pnum) AS "Planets", COUNT(*) AS "Confirmed", MIN(disc_year) AS "First Disc.", ROUND(AVG(pl_orbper)::numeric, 1) AS "Avg Period (days)", ROUND(AVG(pl_rade)::numeric, 2) AS "Avg Radius (R⊕)", ROUND(MIN(sy_dist * 3.26156)::numeric, 1) AS "Dist (ly)" FROM exoplanets WHERE sy_pnum >= 3 GROUP BY hostname, sy_pnum ORDER BY sy_pnum DESC, hostname LIMIT 30

panels.title: Orbital Period Distribution (days < 1000)
panels.description: Earth = 365.25 days. Most close-in transiting planets have periods < 10 days.

targets.rawSql: SELECT pl_orbper AS "Orbital Period (days)" FROM exoplanets WHERE pl_orbper IS NOT NULL AND pl_orbper BETWEEN 0.1 AND 1000

panels.title: Equilibrium Temperature Distribution
panels.description: Green band = habitable zone (180–310 K). Earth effective temp ≈ 255 K.

targets.rawSql: SELECT pl_eqt AS "Eq. Temperature (K)" FROM exoplanets WHERE pl_eqt IS NOT NULL AND pl_eqt BETWEEN 100 AND 4500
SELECT sy_dist AS "Distance (parsecs)" FROM exoplanets WHERE sy_dist IS NOT NULL AND sy_dist < 3000

SELECT pl_name AS "Planet", hostname AS "Star", ROUND(pl_eqt::numeric, 0) AS "Eq. Temp (K)", ROUND((pl_eqt - 273.15)::numeric, 0) AS "Temp (°C)", ROUND(pl_orbper::numeric, 3) AS "Period (days)", ROUND(pl_rade::numeric, 2) AS "Radius (R⊕)", disc_year AS "Year" FROM exoplanets WHERE pl_eqt IS NOT NULL ORDER BY pl_eqt DESC LIMIT 20

SELECT pl_name AS "Planet", hostname AS "Star", ROUND(pl_rade::numeric, 2) AS "Radius (R⊕)", ROUND((pl_rade / 11.209)::numeric, 3) AS "Radius (R♃)", ROUND(pl_masse::numeric, 1) AS "Mass (M⊕)", ROUND(pl_orbper::numeric, 2) AS "Period (days)", disc_year AS "Year" FROM exoplanets WHERE pl_rade IS NOT NULL ORDER BY pl_rade DESC LIMIT 20

SELECT pl_name AS "Planet", hostname AS "Star", ROUND(pl_masse::numeric, 1) AS "Mass (M⊕)", ROUND((pl_masse / 317.83)::numeric, 3) AS "Mass (M♃)", ROUND(pl_rade::numeric, 2) AS "Radius (R⊕)", ROUND(pl_orbper::numeric, 2) AS "Period (days)", disc_year AS "Year" FROM exoplanets WHERE pl_masse IS NOT NULL ORDER BY pl_masse DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: ⏳ Longest Orbital Periods — The Cold Outer Worlds
panels.description: For reference: Earth = 365.25 d, Jupiter = 4333 d (11.9 yr), Neptune = 60,190 d (164.8 yr)

targets.rawSql: SELECT pl_name AS "Planet", hostname AS "Star", ROUND(pl_orbper::numeric, 1) AS "Period (days)", ROUND((pl_orbper / 365.25)::numeric, 2) AS "Period (years)", ROUND(pl_rade::numeric, 2) AS "Radius (R⊕)", ROUND(pl_masse::numeric, 1) AS "Mass (M⊕)", ROUND(pl_orbsmax::numeric, 3) AS "Semi-major Axis (AU)", discoverymethod AS "Method", disc_year AS "Year" FROM exoplanets WHERE pl_orbper IS NOT NULL ORDER BY pl_orbper DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: 🌡 Coolest Stars Hosting Exoplanets (M-dwarfs)
panels.description: Red dwarfs (< 3700 K) are the most common stars and frequently host rocky planets in tight orbits

targets.rawSql: SELECT hostname AS "Star", ROUND(MIN(st_teff)::numeric, 0) AS "Star Temp (K)", ROUND(MIN(st_rad)::numeric, 3) AS "Star Radius (R☉)", ROUND(MIN(st_mass)::numeric, 3) AS "Star Mass (M☉)", MAX(sy_pnum) AS "Planets in System", ROUND(MIN(sy_dist * 3.26156)::numeric, 2) AS "Dist (ly)", MIN(disc_year) AS "First Disc." FROM exoplanets WHERE st_teff < 3700 AND st_teff IS NOT NULL GROUP BY hostname ORDER BY MIN(st_teff) ASC LIMIT 25
